cateval 1.4.0
  • Reference

    Estimate Observed vs. Predicted Treatment Benefit by Calibration Group

    compute_overall_benefit.Rd

    This function evaluates how well predicted treatment benefits correspond to observed differences in outcomes. Patients are divided into calibration groups based on predicted benefit, and observed outcome differences between concordant and discordant treatments are regressed on the predicted benefit difference. Concordance is defined either by receiving the top predicted treatment or any within a specified tolerance of the best. Matching is used to adjust for confounding.

    compute_overall_benefit(
  data,
  drug_var,
  outcome_var,
  cal_groups,
  pred_cols = NULL,
  conc_tolerance = NULL,
  matching_var = NULL,
  match.exact = NULL,
  match.antiexact = NULL,
  extract.match.cohort = FALSE
)

    Arguments

    data

    A data frame containing treatment assignments, outcomes, covariates, and predicted benefits.

    drug_var

    Character. Name of the column indicating the actual treatment received.

    outcome_var

    Character. Name of the outcome variable.

    cal_groups

    Integer. Number of calibration groups to divide the population into (e.g., 5 for quintiles).

    pred_cols

    Character vector. Column names of predicted outcomes (or risks) for each treatment. Should share a common prefix (e.g., "pred_GLP1", "pred_SGLT2").

    conc_tolerance

    Optional numeric. If supplied, defines the absolute tolerance for determining concordance with the best predicted treatment. If NULL, concordance is based on the top predicted treatment only.

    matching_var

    Character vector. Covariate names used for Mahalanobis distance matching.

    match.exact

    Optional character vector. Variables required to match exactly across groups. The best predicted treatment is always included.

    match.antiexact

    Optional character vector. Variables for anti-exact matching. The actual treatment variable is always included.

    extract.match.cohort

    Optional logical. If TRUE, returns matched population used for calculating overall benefits.

    Value

    A data frame with one row per calibration group, containing:

    mean

    Average predicted benefit in the group (discordant minus concordant).

    coef

    Estimated observed difference in outcome (regression coefficient).

    coef_low

    Lower bound of the 95% confidence interval.

    coef_high

    Upper bound of the 95% confidence interval.

    n_groups

    Total number of calibration groups.

    n

    Number of matched pairs in each group.

    Details

    The function uses MatchIt for nearest-neighbor matching to control confounding. Concordant patients are those who received their predicted-optimal treatment, while discordant patients received a suboptimal one. Within each calibration group, observed outcomes are compared using regression.

    Contents

    Developed by Pedro Cardoso.

    Site built with pkgdown 2.0.7.
